item.title: Metro Vancouver Regional District to implement Stage 3 water restrictions
item.description: The Metro Vancouver Regional District is further cracking down on outdoor water use, with Stage 3 water restrictions to be implemented on June 8 amid hot and dry weather in the region.

evaluation.reason_en: This is a high-urgency, high-impact local policy change directly affecting daily life for all Metro Vancouver residents, with clear rules and dates, sourced from CBC.

item.title: Where to watch FIFA World Cup games in Metro Vancouver
item.description: From Hastings Park to The Shipyards, cities and venues across the region are hosting public watch parties for the FIFA World Cup 2026.

evaluation.reason_en: This is a timely, locally sourced CBC guide to public FIFA World Cup 2026 watch parties across Metro Vancouver, highly relevant to the Persian community's strong interest in soccer and social gathering opportunities.

item.title: What a recent B.C. court decision could mean for remote work
item.description: As more workers across the country find themselves forced back into the office full time, experts say a recent court decision in British Columbia could have broader implications for remote work — and shows why employers need to implement return-to-office mandates with care.

evaluation.reason_en: A BC Appeal Court decision on remote work has direct implications for Vancouver workers, especially immigrants and newcomers navigating workplace rights in a new country, and CBC is a highly trustworthy source.
evaluation.persian_angle: این تصمیم دادگاه بریتیش کلمبیا درباره کار از راه دور می‌تواند مستقیماً بر زندگی کاری ایرانیان ونکووری تأثیر بگذارد، به‌ویژه کسانی که تازه وارد بازار کار کانادا شده‌اند و باید حقوق خود را بشناسند.

item.title: Virus detected in invasive mosquitoes found in B.C.'s Sea-to-Sky region
item.description: Researchers found two mosquito species that tested positive for California serogroup virus, a group of mosquito-borne viruses that can cause encephalitis and meningitis in humans.

evaluation.reason_en: CBC reports invasive mosquitoes carrying California serogroup virus found in BC's Sea-to-Sky region, which is relevant for outdoor safety awareness for Vancouver-area residents and newcomers who may not know local health risks.
evaluation.persian_angle: این خبر به ایرانی‌های ونکوور کمک می‌کند تا در مورد خطرات پشه‌های مهاجم و ویروس‌های منتقل‌شونده در منطقه سیتای اسکای آگاه شوند، به‌ویژه خانواده‌ها و تازه‌واردانی که ممکن است با خطرات بهداشتی محلی آشنا نباشند.

item.description: CUPE 1622 members work at animal hospitals and shelters, and as front-line staff, such as protection officers.

item.description: The move is the latest in an overhaul that has seen Vancouver replace general manager Patrik Allvin, president of hockey operations Jim Rutherford, and head coach Adam Foote since finishing the season last in the league with a 25-29-8 record.

evaluation.reason_en: This is a credible, locally relevant sports story about a beloved Vancouver team, but it offers little practical value or actionable information for immigrants and newcomers trying to navigate daily life in Metro Vancouver.

item.title: Decades after it was confiscated, a 1930s Japanese Canadian fishing boat is being restored in Delta
item.description: A historic fishing boat that reflects the impact of Japanese internment on B.C.'s fishing industry is nearing the end of a long restoration project in Delta — but the community group behind it says the vessel still has no permanent home.

evaluation.reason_en: A well-sourced CBC story about a Delta heritage restoration project with mild immigrant-community resonance through its internment theme, but low practical usefulness and urgency for daily life.

item.title: Metro Vancouver board chair promises 'completely open book' review of North Shore wastewater treatment plant
item.description: The Metro Vancouver Regional District has announced that an independent review of the $3.86-billion North Shore Wastewater Plant will resume its work, after litigation with the previous developer on the controversial project was settled.

item.title: Vancouver soccer fan questions FIFA ticket practices as the federation faces increased scrutiny globally
item.description: A North Vancouver soccer fan says FIFA should have been more transparent about where fans could end up sitting after he paid nearly $1,000 for a World Cup ticket. It comes as attorneys general in New York and New Jersey have subpoenaed FIFA as part of an investigation into ticketing practices for the 2026 World Cup.
